title: Psalms, Chapter 99
description: Psalms chapter 99. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 114. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 114.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>ψαλμοϲ ειϲ εξομολογηϲιν αλαλαξατε τω κω παϲα η γη <sup>2</sup>δουλευϲατε τω κω εν ευφροϲυνη ειϲελθετε ενωπιον αυτου εν αγαλλιαϲι <sup>3</sup>γνωτε οτι κϲ αυτοϲ εϲτιν ο θϲ αυτοϲ εποιηϲεν ημαϲ και ουχ ημιϲ λαοϲ αυτου και προβατα τηϲ νομηϲαυτου <sup>4</sup>ειϲελθετε ειϲ ταϲ πυλαϲ αυτου εν εξομολογηϲει ειϲ ταϲ αυλαϲ αυτου εν υμνοιϲ εξομολογειϲθαι αυτω αινειται το ονομα αυτου <sup>5</sup>οτι χρηϲτοϲ κϲ ειϲ τον αιωνα το ελεοϲ αυτου και εωϲ γενεαϲ και γενεαϲ η αληθια αυτου
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>The LORD reigneth; let the people tremble: he sitteth
between
 the cherubims; let the earth be moved. <sup>2</sup>The LORD
is great in Zion; and he
is high above all the people. <sup>3</sup>Let them praise thy great and terrible name;
for it
is
 holy. <sup>4</sup>The king’s strength also loveth judgment; thou dost establish equity, thou executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob. <sup>5</sup>Exalt ye the LORD our God, and worship at his footstool;
for
 he
is holy.

<sup>6</sup>Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name; they called upon the LORD, and he answered them. <sup>7</sup>He spake unto them in the cloudy pillar: they kept his testimonies, and the ordinance
that he gave them. <sup>8</sup>Thou answeredst them, O LORD our God: thou wast a God that forgavest them, though thou tookest vengeance of their inventions. <sup>9</sup>Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his holy hill; for the LORD our God
is holy.
